commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Claude live-session control_request/can_use_tool handling, maps allow/deny decisions from the resolved exec policy, and adds spawn-path regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows Claude live control_request frames hit the non-result early return without any control_response path, matching the linked stall report.
commands.review_followup: A focused repair can preserve explicit permission modes, narrow MCP auto-allow scope, and update regression tests on the PR branch.

target.title: fix(agents): bridge Claude native tools through exec policy
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #80819.

Claude CLI live sessions can emit stream-json `control_request` frames for native tool permission checks. OpenClaw previously did not answer those frames, so Claude could wait until the no-output timeout and the session looked stuck.

This PR makes the live-session bridge answer `can_use_tool` requests and mirrors OpenClaw's resolved exec policy for Claude's native manager tools.

## What Changed

- Adds Claude live `control_request` / `can_use_tool` handling.
- Resolves the effective OpenClaw exec policy once per Claude live session, using agent-level `tools.exec`, then global `tools.exec`, then OpenClaw defaults.
- Allows known Claude manager-native tools when the effective exec policy is `security: "full", ask: "off"`.
- Keeps restrictive exec policies denied with a structured `behavior: "deny"` response instead of leaving Claude waiting.
- Returns a structured error for unsupported control request subtypes.
- Includes the control-request policy in the live-session fingerprint so policy changes create a fresh session.

Allowed manager-native tools currently include `Bash`, file read/write/edit tools, search/list helpers, notebook tools, `Task`, `TodoWrite`, web helpers, and `mcp__*` tools.

## Why

The earlier fix handled native `Bash` only. In practice, OpenClaw Manager sessions need Claude's native file tools too, especially `Write`, `Edit`, and `MultiEdit`. When OpenClaw has already granted the agent full/no-ask local execution, forcing those native tools through denial/fallback paths makes Claude less capable than the surrounding OpenClaw Manager runtime.

This still fails closed for non-full policies.

## Validation

Rebased onto current upstream `main`.

Local checks after rebase:

```
pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts -t control_request
# Test Files  2 passed (2)
# Tests       8 passed | 92 skipped (100)

pnpm tsgo:core:test
# passed
```

Live runtime proof from Thig's OpenClaw deployment after hotpatch + restart:

- Model: `claude-cli/claude-opus-4-7`
- Prompt forced Claude native `Write`, not `Bash`
- Claude emitted native `Write`
- Bridge returned a successful tool result
- File was created with expected content

Created file:

```
/root/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/claude-native-write-probe-20260518T1042.txt
OPENCLAW_CLAUDE_NATIVE_WRITE_OK_1042
```

## Security Notes

- The allow path is gated on OpenClaw's effective exec policy being `security: "full", ask: "off"`.
- Restrictive policies still deny native tool use.
- Malformed requests and unsupported subtypes do not implicitly allow.
- No new network endpoint or credential handling is introduced.
